evals.prompt: I want to build a personal finance tracker. Something simple — I just want to log my daily expenses and see where my money goes each month. Maybe some charts. I haven't really thought through the details yet, can you help me think through this?
evals.expected_output: The skill should enter INITIALIZE mode (no SPEC.md exists). It should ask 2-3 guided questions about the core problem, target user, and main scenarios. After the conversation, it should produce a SPEC.md with frontmatter, core problem, target users, core scenarios with acceptance criteria, key design decisions, scope boundaries, and pending decisions. It should also output a completeness summary.
expectations: Creates a SPEC.md file with the standard frontmatter containing 'design-reference' status and warning
SPEC.md contains a 'Core Problem' section
SPEC.md contains a 'Target Users' section
SPEC.md contains at least 2 core scenarios with acceptance criteria
SPEC.md contains a 'Key Design Decisions' section with reasoning (because/why)
SPEC.md contains an 'Explicitly Out of Scope' section
Outputs a completeness check summary showing which areas are covered vs pending
Asks the user questions in a guided, progressive manner rather than dumping all questions at once

evals.prompt:
I want to add a feature to export highlights as a weekly email digest. The user should be able to configure which day of the week they get the digest, and it should include their top 5 most recent highlights with source links.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: The current working directory contains the mock project files. The project root is the current directory. SPEC.md already exists at ./SPEC.md. The project code is in ./src/ and package.json is at ./package.json.
evals.expected_output: The skill should enter EVOLVE mode (SPEC.md exists + new feature described). First, it should do a local review of relevant SPEC sections against the actual code, detect that the tech stack has drifted (SPEC says Express+SQLite but code shows Next.js+Prisma), report this to the user, then proceed to shape the email digest feature. It should incrementally update the SPEC, not rewrite it entirely.
expectations: Detects that SPEC.md exists and enters EVOLVE mode, not INITIALIZE mode
Performs a local review before evolving: reads relevant SPEC sections and scans code files
Detects tech stack drift: SPEC says Express+SQLite but code shows Next.js+Prisma+next-auth
Reports the drift to the user and proposes SPEC updates before proceeding
After drift resolution, asks targeted questions about the email digest feature
Updates SPEC incrementally rather than rewriting the entire file
The updated SPEC preserves the frontmatter warning header

evals.prompt:
Can you review my current SPEC and see how it matches up with what I've actually built?

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: The current working directory contains the mock project files. The project root is the current directory. SPEC.md already exists at ./SPEC.md. The project code is in ./src/ and package.json is at ./package.json.
evals.expected_output: The skill should enter REVIEW mode (SPEC.md exists + user said 'review'). It should read the full SPEC, scan the code, and produce a structured difference report organized into three categories: planned but not built, built but not documented, and changed. It should then ask the user how to handle each finding.
expectations: Detects REVIEW mode from the user's 'review' request
Reads the full SPEC.md file
Scans code files: package.json, route definitions, schema/models
Identifies 'Changed' items: Express→Next.js, SQLite→Prisma, EJS→React, email/password→next-auth
Identifies 'Built but not documented': Kindle import endpoint, Tag model, Highlight notes field
Organizes findings into clear categories (planned-not-built, built-not-documented, changed)
Asks the user how to handle each finding rather than auto-updating
